entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated brain builds a self-wiring knowledge graph by automatically extracting entities and relationships from agent interactions. It utilizes a hybrid search mechanism that combines vector retrieval with backlink-boosted ranking, supported by 26 modular skills for data ingestion and enrichment.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements biomimetic data structures to facilitate long-term agent learning through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It allows Hermes to organize facts into evolving mental models while maintaining compatibility with major providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses a multi-agent loop to propose strategies and curate knowledge based on past performance. It specializes in distilling validated execution data into optimized runtimes, allowing agents to improve their behavior across repeated complex scenarios.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: This plugin provides a lossless context engine that stores messages in an immutable SQLite DAG to ensure no data is ever deleted. It manages long histories through a three-level hierarchical summary system, offering specialized tools for agents to grep and expand compacted context.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This self-hosted backend enables private, cross-session memory for Hermes Agent using a stack of Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis. It is designed for users who require full control over their data while using any OpenAI-compatible or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first memory vault that uses a hybrid retrieval approach combining vector search, BM25, and graph traversal. It focuses on on-device persistence, automatically capturing agent decisions and generating handoffs without relying on cloud dependencies.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer that stores persistent data as open-format YAML engrams to facilitate multi-agent interoperability. It features a zero-API-cost retrieval system that automatically decays or strengthens memories based on how frequently the agent accesses them.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This MCP-native server provides anticipatory memory by prefetching relevant context from session logs before the agent explicitly requests it. Built with SQLite-vec and node-llama-cpp, it offers a local-first RAG solution optimized for coding agent workflows.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry standards like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables Hermes to operate across 26 security domains, including malware analysis and cloud security, using the agentskills.io integration standard.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI patterns using a 109-entry word replacement table across three severity tiers. It ensures Hermes Agent outputs remain human-like through a two-pass audit process that catches surviving AI signatures.
entries.wondelai/skills: A modular, cross-platform library that provides Hermes with specialized frameworks for architecture, UX design, and product strategy. Skills are easily managed via an npx CLI and are fully compatible with the agentskills.io standard for seamless agent integration.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI, offering type-safe skill definitions for Hermes. It utilizes progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token usage while supporting both filesystem and programmatic skill loading.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate professional draw.io diagrams, such as UML and flowcharts, directly from natural language descriptions. It features grid-aligned layouts and animated connectors, making it ideal for visualizing complex system architectures.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Built on the agentskills.io spec, this library enables Hermes to interact with Chainlink oracle networks and smart contracts. It supports multi-chain data feeds, cross-chain sends via CCIP, and local testing workflows for blockchain-enabled agents.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill allows Hermes to weave runnable code and prose together into .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract source files and automatically syncs edits back to the primary documentation, maintaining a single source of truth.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories to track work history. It features an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality data to train and evaluate replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: This registry and SDK facilitate the lifecycle management of reusable AI skills with built-in support for Stripe and TON blockchain monetization. It automates dependency resolution and local installation, providing a standardized way for Hermes to discover new capabilities.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide Hermes with standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 image generation models. They support precise hex color specifications and structured JSON prompting for high-fidelity visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills for Hermes. It generates both Python scaffolds and SKILL.md documentation, allowing the agent to expand its own capabilities through observation.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a local-first harness that manages long-running missions for Hermes through structured planning and validation assertions. It uses a .maestro directory for state management and generates markdown briefs to facilitate seamless handoffs between agent runs.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill equips Hermes with the ability to execute complex, multi-step tasks autonomously with built-in checkpoints. It features real-time progress tracking and rollback procedures to ensure safe recovery if a task encounters an error.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses three-layer lazy loading to minimize token consumption during skill discovery. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading of configurations without downtime.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: This forge converts existing repositories and developer documentation into agentskills.io-compliant instruction sets. It maps AI instructions to specific source code line numbers, allowing Hermes to gain deep, version-pinned expertise in specific libraries.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community-driven hub for browsing and installing Hermes skills with automated security scanning for prompt injection. It features a micropayment protocol for premium content and an agent-to-agent feedback system based on proof-of-use reviews.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem, allowing Hermes to utilize C# based skills via the MCP SDK. It implements progressive disclosure to save tokens and supports automatic skill discovery from standard agent directories.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily-updated Nix packages for a wide range of AI coding agents, ensuring reproducible environments for both open-source and proprietary tools. It is the ideal choice for developers who require a stable, version-controlled dependency chain for their Hermes workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Focusing on high-security environments, this project implements hardware-enforced sandboxing through NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking. It offers pre-configured security policies and a library of over 40 integrated tools, making it the most robust option for sensitive agent operations.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, systemd services, and secure secrets via sops-nix. It bridges the gap between automated infrastructure and manual customization by supporting both Nix-managed and interactive CLI skill installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this no-install desktop agent provides a GUI and over 100 tools without requiring Docker or administrative rights. It features an integrated workflow engine for scheduled automation and built-in support for local models and GPU monitoring.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This repository offers a streamlined Docker-based sandbox that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It is built for simplicity, supporting persistent state through mounted directories and the ability to target specific Hermes tags during the build process.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production-ready headless deployments on Ubuntu and Debian, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is specifically optimized for autonomous execution, enabling the agent to handle recurring AI cron jobs and Nous Portal integrations.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal, allowing users to interact with the agent directly through a browser. It is designed for easy orchestration via Portainer and utilizes GitHub Actions for automated image builds and container management.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen with zero external dependencies. It is essential for Hermes users who require a private environment to monitor task spend and implement human-in-the-loop sign-offs via the Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw provides a self-hosted runtime for building autonomous agent swarms that support 23 different model providers, including Hermes and local LLMs. It distinguishes itself through persistent agent memory and a unique 'conversation-to-skill' learning mechanism that expands agent capabilities over time.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized framework focuses on meta-optimization by using an automated outer-loop search to improve the performance of Hermes Agent on benchmarks. It orchestrates structured candidate mutations to find the most efficient harness code for TBLite and TB2 evaluation tasks.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars in real-time. The system orchestrates nine specialized agents and visualizes their tasks, code generation, and scoreboard metrics through a live dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This framework coordinates 17 specialized agents across distinct development phases ranging from research and planning to infrastructure. It ensures high-quality outputs by implementing mandatory quality gates and code reviews at every stage of the multi-agent lifecycle.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It enforces architectural integrity through an eight-phase process where each transition is protected by automated graph-diff phase gates.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This project enables ACP-style delegation by routing tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution. It prioritizes safety and efficiency through isolated workflows, configurable timeouts, and strict output character limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: A Rust-based CLI tool that monitors token consumption across over 20 AI coding agents and CLIs. It provides real-time cost tracking via LiteLLM data and features a unique 3D visualization dashboard for usage analysis.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This project offers community-driven visual customizations for the Hermes terminal interface. Users can define custom ASCII banners, spinners, and color schemes using YAML-based configurations that allow for partial theme overrides.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: A specialized port that integrates the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel. It runs natively without OAuth or external processes and includes integrated MLOps tools for inference and RL training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It enables durable session management with checkpoints and provides a supervisory sidebar for human auditing and intervention.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: A framework for building Task-Agnostic Workflow (TAW) agents that operate within specific project scopes. It allows for per-folder configurations, enabling seamless switching between default and scoped agents with local memories and tools.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A zero-latency static linter designed to identify structural bugs in AI agent configurations and system prompts. It uses seven specialized detectors to provide automated quality gating for CI/CD pipelines without requiring LLM calls or API keys.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: A dedicated migration utility that facilitates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It automates the mapping of model configurations, personas, and memories, while also handling the transfer of local and system-level credentials.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: An automated deployment tool that spins up a complete Hermes Agent stack using Docker, LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant. It provides a zero-cost environment featuring 29 community plugins and pre-configured routing for free local models.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-build web interface provides full feature parity with the Hermes CLI through a streamlined three-panel layout for chat, file browsing, and session management. It is designed for accessibility across both desktop and mobile browsers using a lightweight Python and vanilla JavaScript architecture.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A security-hardened native web workspace that features real-time SSE streaming for agent outputs and tool call rendering. It offers comprehensive administrative control over agent memory, skills, and background jobs, protected by execution approval prompts and authentication middleware.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted dashboard acts as a multi-agent gateway for configuring and monitoring multiple agent profiles simultaneously. It distinguishes itself with detailed token analytics that track costs by model and platform alongside a browser-based terminal and system metrics panel.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that establishes direct SSH connections to agents without requiring middle-layer APIs or remote daemons. It provides a multi-tabbed terminal environment for managing remote cron jobs, skills, and SQLite session stores directly from the desktop.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This React-based Telegram Mini App offers a mobile-first dashboard featuring a terminal-style chat interface with slash command support. It enables users to spawn background agents and monitor live output alongside integrated system health gauges for CPU and memory usage.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A desktop companion app focused on simplifying the lifecycle of Hermes Agent through guided installation and automated dependency resolution. It supports a wide array of LLM providers and includes integrated messaging gateways for platforms like Discord and Telegram.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: This monitoring-centric dashboard specializes in process tracking and cost distribution analysis for Hermes sessions. It features deep conversation inspection powered by FTS5 search and provides a read-only interface for viewing configurations and scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that integrates a marketplace for skills, MCP servers, and plugins to extend agent capabilities. It utilizes isolated profile management to keep sessions, memory, and API keys separate while providing a visual timeline of tool executions.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: Featuring a glassmorphic design, this single-file HTML interface uses a Python proxy to deliver real-time previews of HTML, SVG, and PDF artifacts. It combines a modern aesthetic with functional tools for inspecting local agent memory and file directories.
